theoretical training with the gold
of practical experience to make a

This is what May Arkwright Hut
ton told the girls who yesterday
competed for the three prizes offer
ed for the best essay in behalf of
equal suffrage. If all the scoffers at
the equal suffrage question as a live
issue in this state had been present
there would be few such scoffers
today. The high school auditorium,
which is said to hold $500 people,
was full of enthusiastic listeners.
The prizes were awarded by the
judges, Mrs. R. A. Hutchinson, Mrs.
A. l.. Wright and Mrs. George
Turner, as follows: Miss Nellie
Hemingway, $40; Miss Clare
Giboney. $40; Miss Georgia Parker,
SIS. There were six contestants.
and in creditable manner they pre-.
sented some of the credit due
womankind for the big things Of
the worlds history that is not al
ways remembered. Mrs. George
Turner remarked that she had
heard many men compete for ora
torical prizes and that they failed
to set the same standard as that
set by the Spokane high SchOo!
girls yesterday.


Mrs. Hutton announced that at
the beginning of, the next school
term a similar contest would be
held for boys only, proposition
